sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच
एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेशः परन्तपः।
न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह।।
transliteration:
sañjaya uvāca
evam uktvā hṛṣīkeśaṁ guḍākeśaḥ parantapaḥ
na yotsya iti govindam uktvā tūṣṇīṁ babhūva ha
literal_meaning: Sañjaya said: Having spoken thus to Hṛṣīkeśa, the thick-haired (Arjuna), chastiser of foes, declared to Govinda 'I shall not fight,' and fell silent.
contextual_meaning: After a cascade of anxious arguments, Arjuna collapses into mute withdrawal, surrendering agency to Kṛṣṇa and signaling readiness for inner guidance.
psychological_state: Acute stress-induced shutdown—dissociative silence following hyper-verbal conflict; entry into receptive, client-like stance toward the therapist-guru.
trigger_scenario: An over-functioning team-leader suddenly goes blank in a crisis meeting, mutely handing the projector remote to the coach, finally ready to listen.
anchor_text: When words fail, silence invites true guidance.
